description: Exports the data table to a new raster file. Use the COPY command with FORMAT RASTER.
example: COPY ( SELECT geometry, databand_1, databand_2, databand_3 FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif') ) TO 'path/to/raster/newfile.tif' WITH (FORMAT RASTER, DRIVER 'COG', CREATION_OPTIONS ('COMPRESS=LZW'), SRS 'EPSG:25830');

description: Packages a plain SQL array of numeric values back into a datacube BLOB, the inverse of RT_Cube2Array.
example: SELECT RT_Array2Cube(r.values, 'RAW', r.bands, r.cols, r.rows, r.no_data) FROM ...;

description: Returns the value in a specified band of a datacube at the specified world coordinates (x, y).
example: SELECT RT_CoordValue(databand_1, 0, 545600.0, 4724500.0, metadata, -9999.0) AS value FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Returns the value in a set of datacubes at the specified world coordinates (x, y).
example: SELECT RT_CoordValue_Agg(databand_1, 0, 545600.0, 4724500.0, tile_x, tile_y, metadata, -9999.0) FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Returns the values in a band of a datacube at the specified array of world coordinates (x, y).

Extracts the pixel values of a datacube column into a plain SQL array of a chosen numeric type. Optionally filters out nodata values. A different function variant is provided for each numeric data type (e.g. RT_Cube2ArrayInt32, RT_Cube2ArrayFloat, etc.).
example: SELECT RT_Cube2ArrayInt32(databand_1, true) AS r FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Changes the pixel data type of a datacube, returning a new datacube of the same dimensions. A different function variant is provided for each numeric data type (e.g. RT_Cube2TypeUInt8, RT_Cube2TypeFloat, etc.).
Returns a datacube with the absolute value of each cell. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the sum of the two inputs. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where cells inside the given geometry are replaced by the specified value. No-data cells are preserved.
example: SELECT RT_CubeBurn(databand_1, tile_x, tile_y, metadata, ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON((...))'), 1.0) FROM ...;

description: Returns a datacube where cells outside the given geometry are replaced by the specified value. No-data cells are preserved.
example: SELECT RT_CubeClip(databand_1, tile_x, tile_y, metadata, ST_GeomFromText('POLYGON((...))'), 0.0) FROM ...;

description: Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the left-hand cell divided by the right-hand cell. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left == right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with the exponential (e^x) of each cell. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where all cells (including nodata) are unconditionally replaced by the right-hand value.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left > right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left >= right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left < right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left <= right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with the natural logarithm of each cell. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the maximum of the two inputs. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the minimum of the two inputs. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the remainder of dividing the left-hand cell by the right-hand value. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the product of the two inputs. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube with each cell negated (multiplied by -1). Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where each cell is 1 if left != right, 0 otherwise. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns true if the datacube is NULL or contains only nodata cells, false otherwise.
Returns a datacube with each cell raised to the power of the right-hand value. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns a datacube where valid cells are replaced by the right-hand value. Nodata cells in the source are preserved.
Returns a datacube where nodata cells are replaced by the specified value, and sets this value as the new nodata sentinel.
Returns a datacube with the square root of each cell. Nodata cells are preserved.
Calculates statistics for a specific band (0-based index) of a datacube. Returns a STRUCT with minimum, maximum, sum, mean, stddev, valid_count and nodata_count fields.
example: SELECT RT_CubeStats(databand_1, 0) AS stats FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Calculates statistics for a specific band (0-based index) in a set of datacubes. Returns a STRUCT with minimum, maximum, sum, mean, stddev, valid_count and nodata_count fields.
example: SELECT RT_CubeStats_Agg(databand_1, 0) AS stats FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Returns a datacube with each cell equal to the left-hand cell minus the right-hand cell. Inputs can be two datacubes or a datacube and a scalar. Nodata cells are preserved.
Returns the list of supported GDAL raster drivers and file formats.
Computes the bounding box of the valid (non-no-data) cells in the input datacube for a specific band and returns it as a geometry.
example: SELECT RT_Envelope(databand_1, 0, tile_x, tile_y, metadata) AS geometry FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Sets a GDAL configuration option (equivalent to CPLSetConfigOption). Pass NULL as the value to unset the option.
Creates a polygon geometry for each contiguous region of non-no-data values for a specific band in the datacube.
example: SELECT RT_Polygon(databand_1, 0, tile_x, tile_y, metadata) AS geometry FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Returns the value in a specified band of a datacube at the specified pixel coordinates (column, row).
Returns the value in a set of datacubes at the specified pixel coordinates (column, row).
example: SELECT RT_RasterValue_Agg(databand_1, 0, 0, 20, tile_x, tile_y, metadata, -9999.0) FROM RT_Read('path/to/raster/file.tif');

description: Returns the values in a band of a datacube at the specified array of pixel coordinates (column, row).
Opens a raster file (or a mosaic of raster files) and returns a table with the raster data. Supports filter pushdown on all non-BLOB columns.
Reads a raster file (or a mosaic of raster files) and returns a table with one row per value cell in the raster.
example: SELECT id, x, y, geometry, col, row, band_1, band_2, band_3 FROM RT_ReadCells('path/to/raster/file.tif');
